title: HRBP Finance and Procurement Services
description:
Category: Human Resources
Type: Full time

Job Title: HRBP Finance and Procurement Services

Country / Location: Bangalore

About Unilever

Be part of one of the world’s largest consumer goods companies. We are driven by our purpose to make sustainable living commonplace. We are present in 190 countries, have 400+ brands and 13 brands with turnover of over €1 bn.  Every day, 2.5 bn people use our products every day globally, nine out of ten Indian households use our products daily to feel good, look good and get more out of life – giving us a unique opportunity to build a brighter future.

We have a fantastic future facing Global HRBP opportunity within UniOps (Unilever Operations).  UniOps delivers all the technology, processes, systems, and data that enables Unilever to run. UniOps is transforming.  We power purpose-led growth by delivering future fit operations.

We need best-in-class commercial HR talent. We are looking for commercial business partners who can manage volume, complexity, and have a business centric approach. 

Business Context

In today’s complex and fast-moving world, Unilever needs to be able to respond fast, demonstrate resilience and make smart decisions, faster, better and on a larger scale than our competitors.  Our company’s ability to deliver growth depends on it.

Unilever Operations (UniOps) plays a vital role in this, through reducing complexity and leveraging technologies and data to support global digitalisation and to allow our Markets, Divisions and Functions to focus on growth.

The HRBP role will partner Global Technology Applications, Enterprise Operations, Customer Technology, Employee Experience Technology, Enterprise Architecture and Technology Innovation and Sustainability groups, sitting as an integral member of their Leadership team.    

Purpose of the Role

To act as a strategic HR partner to the Finance Services and Procurement Services leadership teams, enabling delivery of business outcomes through effective organisation design, workforce planning, talent, culture, and change leadership. The role supports global, multi‑location teams operating in a highly digital, process‑driven, and transformation‑heavy environment.

Key Accountabilities

1. Strategic Business Partnering

Partner Finance Services and Procurement Services leadership to translate business strategy into a fit‑for‑purpose people agenda.

Provide forward‑looking HR insight on organisational effectiveness, skills, capacity, cost, and capability impacts.

Act as a trusted advisor on leadership, team effectiveness, and decision‑making.

2. Organisation Design & Workforce Planning

Lead workforce planning cycles, including capacity, skills, location strategy, and cost optimisation.

Support organisation design, role clarity, and span/layer optimisation aligned to GBS and functional strategies.

Partner with Finance to ensure robust people cost forecasting and workforce assumptions.

3. Talent, Performance & Succession

Lead annual and quarterly talent processes (e.g., talent reviews, succession planning, development actions).

Support leaders with performance management, differentiation, and capability uplift.

Partner with Talent and Learning teams to deploy targeted development, reskilling, and future‑capability programmes.

4. Change Management & Transformation

Play a core role in major transformation initiatives (digitalisation, automation, operating‑model changes, vendor shifts).

Provide structured change leadership support: impact assessment, stakeholder engagement, communications, and adoption.

Partner closely with transformation, finance excellence, and procurement operations teams.

5. Culture, Engagement & Employee Relations

Drive a high‑performance, inclusive culture aligned to Unilever values and GBS ways of working.

Lead engagement action planning and follow‑through.

Provide pragmatic ER guidance, working with Employee Relations specialists where required.

6. HR Governance, Data & Service Excellence

Ensure adherence to global HR frameworks, reward principles, and governance standards.

Use people data and insights (Workday, engagement, attrition, skill data) to inform decisions and track impact.

Partner with HR Operations/People Experience teams to ensure high‑quality, seamless employee experience delivery.

Key Stakeholders

Heads of Finance Services and Procurement Services

Finance & Procurement Services leadership teams

GBS Transformation and Strategy teams

HR Expertise teams (Talent, Reward, Learning, ER)

People Experience / HR Operations

Global and regional HRBPs

Skills & Experience

Essential

Strong HR Business Partner experience in Finance, Procurement, Supply Chain, or GBS environments.

Candidates must possess at least 6 years of relevant experience.

Demonstrated experience in organisation design, workforce planning, and talent management.

Proven change management capability in complex, multi‑country settings.

Strong stakeholder influencing skills with senior leaders.

Data‑driven mindset with the ability to turn insight into action.

Desirable

Experience supporting digital, automation, or shared‑services transformations.

Exposure to outsourced / third‑party service models.

Experience working in global, matrixed organisations.

Critical Success Factors

Ability to balance strategic partnership with strong operational execution

Commercial acumen and understanding of Finance and Procurement operating models

Comfort working in ambiguity and fast‑paced transformation environments

High credibility with senior leaders through judgement, insight, and delivery

Our commitment to Equality, Diversity & Inclusion
Unilever embraces diversity and encourages applicants from all walks of life! This means giving full and fair consideration to all applicants and continuing development of all employees regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, and pregnancy and maternity.
Additional Information
Please apply via this portal as e-mail applications will not be processed. By applying to this role, you are confirming that you have informed your Line Manager and that you are at a point in your career with Unilever where it is appropriate for you to be applying for other positions.

We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment journey. Should you require additional information or reasonable adjustments to allow you to present your best self during this process, please contact your Talent Partner listed on the role.

title: Senior Sales Executive
description:
Category: Customer Development
Type: Full time

Job Position: Senior Sales Executive
Function: OOH
WL: 1B/1C
Reports to: Area Sales Manager     
Location: Delhi

Business Context and Main Purpose of the Job: The Nature of the Job is “Speciality Food Sales”. The candidate will work in HORECA channel (Hotels, Restaurants, Caterers) and meet target customer group like Chefs, Purchase Managers, Owners etc) every day, Manage the Distributors of the given geography, Explore the territory and grow the existing business as per given guidelines.

Main Accountabilities​:

Key responsibilities: -

Develop the Horeca & Corporate channel focusing on the hotels/cafe/restaurants sector for sales & business development for OOH - Vending

Responsible for identifying and securing new clients in addition to yielding existing clients to ensure revenue targets are met.

Analyse market needs and trends, to identify and deliver strategies to support the company's business development plan.

Develop and maintain a high level of product and service knowledge to facilitate support to potential and existing clients.

Meet the sales targets and KPIs as set and agreed upon by the senior management.

Weekly reporting on all sales activities to monitor the success and effectiveness of activities.

Complete Distributor Management (Stocks, Billing, Inventory, Hygiene, Secondary, ROI)

Competency & Skill Set:

3+ years of experience in sales with a focus on Horeca & Corporates (Hotels/Restaurants/Cafes/Corporates).

Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.

A record of achieving sales targets and increasing revenue.

Able to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities and provide customized solutions.

Self-motivated, hardworking, and able to work independently with a strong sale drive.

Must be willing to travel as needed.

Must be fluent in English and local language.

Able to work independently, professionally and be a good team player.

Good interpersonal and Influential Skills.

Educational Qualification:

MBA/PGDM in Any Specialization/ Hotel Management Graduate

Experience in HORECA in Food/Beverages products sales preferred.

#LI-Remote

title: Key Account Executive - Modern Trade
description:
Category: Customer Development
Type: Full time

Job Title: Key Account Executive

WL- 1B/1C

Location: Mumbai RO

Job Overview:

The role will be a part of the HUL Modern Trade Sales team in a customer facing assignment. He/ She works in tandem with other functions viz. CM, CD-Ops, CD- Finance, CD-Supply Chain & branches. Executive would also assist the KAM in handling the account portfolio.

The right candidate for this role needs to be good at customer relations and maintaining a great and positive working equation with his/her counterparts. The role requires high discipline with timelines and strong business understanding with basic negotiation skills and stakeholder management.

Driving the right mix and activations with the customer through effectively planning the promotions and investments along with the KAM and CSP’s

Ensuring that all operational work is aligned and completed on time with the customers and internal stakeholders.

Ensuring Availability and in-store execution for the business.

Being the voice of the customer in HUL and vice versa would be the key expectation. The person would be responsible for all business transactions between HUL and customers.

Scope of work:

The KAE job by nature requires him/her to generate practical yet creative solutions to improve execution in the accounts. Scope includes innovating in the areas of POSM, Ops, Merchandising, Promotion, & ways of working in his day-to-day job

The person would be responsible for leading and closing all operational work and day to day tasks with the customers which is very critical in ensuring customer delight.

Managing the accounting and claim settlements with the customer to ensure clean accounting.

The KAE manages contact with various key decision makers across the hierarchy in the modern trade accounts that he/she handles. He/She also handles contact with the merchandising partners.

Team Handling: He/She does not have any HUL employees as direct subordinates.

Qualification:

MBA/PGDM degree in Sales/ Marketing with min 3-4 yrs of relevant experience

Strong in inter- personality and out going

Should be target oriented and determined selling skills is required.

Must have a bend towards being data centric and ensuring that the business is driven by data and insights

title: Electrical Executive
description:
Category: Supply Chain
Type: Full time

Job Title: Electrical Executive

Work Location: Mysore Beverages Factory, HUL

Work Level: WL

Job Summary

The Electrical Executive will be responsible for the safe and efficient operation, maintenance, and reliability of all electrical systems and equipment within a manufacturing plant, ensuring compliance with company standards and regulatory requirements.

Job Responsibilities

* **Maintenance & Operations:**
* Oversee and execute preventive, predictive, and corrective maintenance activities for all electrical installations, including power distribution systems, motors, control panels, lighting, and instrumentation.
* Diagnose and troubleshoot electrical malfunctions, power outages, and equipment breakdowns, implementing timely and effective solutions to minimize downtime.
* Monitor the performance of electrical assets, identify potential issues, and recommend improvements for enhanced reliability and energy efficiency.
* Ensure compliance with all electrical safety standards, LOTO procedures, and company EHS policies.
* Supervise and train junior electrical technicians, fostering a culture of safety and continuous improvement.
* Manage spare parts inventory for electrical components and equipment.

* **Project & Improvement:**
* Participate in the planning and execution of electrical projects, including new installations, upgrades, and modifications.
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams (production, mechanical, EHS) to integrate electrical systems seamlessly into overall plant operations.
* Implement energy conservation initiatives and identify opportunities for cost reduction through optimized electrical usage.
* Maintain accurate records of electrical maintenance activities, equipment specifications, and project documentation.

Job Qualifications

* **Education:** Bachelor's degree in Electrical Engineering or Instrumentation Engineering
* **Experience:** Minimum of 3-8 years of experience in electrical maintenance and operations within a manufacturing environment, preferably in the FMCG sector.
* **Technical Skills:**
* Strong knowledge of industrial electrical systems, including high and low voltage distribution, motor control centers (MCCs), PLCs, VFDs, and instrumentation.
* Proficiency in reading and interpreting electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, and technical manuals.
* Hands-on experience with troubleshooting and repair of electrical equipment.
* Familiarity with relevant electrical safety codes and regulations (e.g., IS standards, IEC).
